message:
feat(fcp-registry): Add RaptorQ erasure-coded symbol mirroring for resilient binary distribution

Enables connector binaries to be encoded into RaptorQ fountain-code symbols
and stored across distributed mirrors, allowing full binary reconstruction
from any sufficient subset of symbols even when some mirrors are unreachable.

Key additions:
- mirror_bundle_symbols() encodes a verified connector binary into a symbol
  set using RaptorQ OTI parameters, stores each symbol as a separate object,
  and pins a ConnectorBinarySymbolSet descriptor with source/total counts
- reconstruct_binary_from_symbols() decodes the binary from available symbols
  with SHA-256 hash validation against the original manifest
- reconstruct_bundle_from_symbol_descriptor() provides full roundtrip recovery
  of both manifest and binary from the symbol layer alone
- New error variants for encode/decode failures, incomplete symbol sets,
  hash mismatches, and target mismatches give clear diagnostics
- ConnectorBinarySymbolSet, SymbolMirrorResult, and ReconstructedConnectorBinary
  types model the full symbol lifecycle
- Integration tests verify: roundtrip encoding/decoding, reconstruction from
  partial symbol subsets using RaptorQ repair symbols, hash mismatch rejection,
  manifest tamper detection, and full bundle recovery

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
